Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists PSY1 as Solyc03g031860.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Paper title/abstract/results identify PSY1 as a phytoene synthase isoform; 'phytoene synthase 1' is directly supported by gene name.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is provided anywhere in the paper.

Source_Species.reason: The study organism is tomato throughout the paper.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Materials and methods state tomato is Solanum lycopersicum var. MicroTom.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Materials and methods specify MicroTom background/variety.

Applied_Species.reason: Experiments were performed in tomato plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Materials and methods specify Solanum lycopersicum.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: PSY catalyzes the first step of the carotenoid pathway, and results show PSY1 affects carotenoid-derived ABA production in pericarp.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results show PSY1 supplies carotenoid production in petals/fruit and ABA precursors in pericarp; this is a fair summary of its upstream role.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Abstract/discussion describe PSY as the first step and main flux-controlling step of carotenoid biosynthesis, i.e. committed/upstream.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: PSY1 is presented as supplying phytoene/carotenoid precursors rather than catalyzing terminal ABA formation.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Abstract and discussion explicitly call PSY the 'first and main flux-controlling step' / 'main rate-determining step'.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Figure 1A/discussion refer to GGPP as the precursor entering the carotenoid pathway through PSY; PSY reaction from GGPP to phytoene is inferable.

Primary_Product.reason: Abstract/results/discussion repeatedly state PSY supplies phytoene.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper identifies PSY as phytoene synthase acting from GGPP to phytoene; condensation of two GGPP molecules is standard and inferable from the pathway scheme.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: PSY1 is directly linked in results to carotenoids and to ABA in pericarp.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Carotenoids are discussed as pigments and ABA as a phytohormone.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper states carotenoids function in photoprotection/pigmentation and ABA regulates fruit growth and ripening-associated processes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results directly test leaves, petals, fruit pericarp, and pericarp ABA pool for PSY1 effects.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PSY1 is a phytoene synthase in the plastidial carotenoid/isoprenoid pathway per abstract/discussion.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results/discussion and supplemental references indicate PSY1 is abundant in shoots, high in flowers/pericarp especially after MG, induced by HL, and little/no expression in mature seed embryos is discussed.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Applied species is the same MicroTom tomato used throughout, so same expression summary applies.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Carotenoids are described as plastidial isoprenoids and PSY functions in chloroplasts/chromoplasts, i.e. plastids.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Discussion states SlG2 can interact with both PSY1 and PSY2.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: All components are directly supported in results: major role in petals/ripe pericarp carotenoids, secondary HL leaf role, and pericarp ABA affecting fruit growth.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used in results to assess PSY1 phenotypes except germination, which was measured mainly for seed ABA/PSY2 but involved psy1 controls; overall supported.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: HL, MG/B/B+6 stages, and salt stress with no PSY1 root ABA role are all directly reported.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each numbered finding matches the results: altered flower/fruit pigmentation, modest leaf photoprotection contribution, reduced pericarp ABA with smaller fruit and slower color development.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a forward-looking application inference, not directly tested in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Loss of PSY1 reduced ripe fruit pigmentation and fruit size/volume; modest HL photoprotection reduction is supported by reduced NPQ/Fv/Fm effects.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results directly show PSY1 is dispensable singly for seedling greening, double loss with PSY2 is albino lethal, and residual carotenoids in psy1 ripe fruit include a PSY2-supported beta-carotene component.

corrections.reason: No EC number is provided anywhere in the paper.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists PSY2 as Solyc02g081330.

Enzyme_Name.reason: PSY2 is one of the phytoene synthase isoforms; 'phytoene synthase 2' is supported by nomenclature.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number appears in the paper.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Materials and methods specify Solanum lycopersicum var. MicroTom.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: PSY2 is directly tested in carotenoid biosynthesis and carotenoid-derived ABA production in seeds/roots.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results support an upstream role supplying chloroplast carotenoids and tissue-specific ABA precursors, especially seeds and roots.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: PSY is described as the first committed/upstream step in carotenoid biosynthesis.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: PSY2 supplies precursors rather than terminal ABA synthesis enzymes.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Paper explicitly states PSY catalyzes the first and main flux-controlling/rate-determining step.

Primary_Substrate.reason: GGPP is shown as substrate entering the carotenoid pathway through PSY in Fig. 1A; same inference applies to PSY2.

Primary_Product.reason: PSY2 is discussed as supplying phytoene.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: As a PSY isoform, PSY2 synthesizes phytoene from GGPP; supported by pathway description/inference.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results link PSY2 to carotenoids and ABA.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Carotenoid and phytohormone classes are supported by text.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper directly connects carotenoids to photoprotection/pigmentation and ABA to seed dormancy and salt-stress responses.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Leaves, petals, fruit pericarp, seeds, and salt-stressed roots are all directly assessed for PSY2 contributions.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PSY2 is a phytoene synthase in the plastidial carotenoid pathway.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results/discussion show PSY2 is high in leaves, induced by HL, present in flowers/seeds, decreases in mature seeds, and is lower than PSY1 in ripening pericarp.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same species/variety as source; same expression summary applies.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: PSY functions in plastids/chloroplasts/chromoplasts according to paper context.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Discussion explicitly states SlG2 can interact with both PSY1 and PSY2.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Directly supported by results: major leaf photoprotection role, modest petal/fruit carotenoid role, principal role in seed and salt-stressed root ABA.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used in the study for PSY2-related phenotypes.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: HL, fruit developmental stages, salt stress 200 mM NaCl for 24 h, and FSM/NFZ treatment of MG fruits are all explicitly described.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three numbered findings match the results figures and text.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an inferred application, not directly tested in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Loss of PSY2 reduces photoprotection/photosynthetic efficiency, accelerates seed germination, and abolishes root ABA increase under salt stress.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results show double psy1 psy2 causes albino lethality and seed dormancy depends specifically on ABA made in mature seeds from PSY2-derived carotenoids.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists PSY3 as Solyc01g005940.

Enzyme_Name.reason: PSY3 is identified as the third phytoene synthase isoform.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number appears in the paper.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Materials and methods specify Solanum lycopersicum var. MicroTom.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: MicroTom is explicitly the background used.

Applied_Species.reason: All experimental work is in tomato.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: PSY3 is discussed as a PSY isoform in carotenoid/root apocarotenoid contexts, but it was not directly functionally tested in this pathway by mutant analysis in this study.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Root-associated branch is discussed based on expression and prior literature, not directly established by core experiments here.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: As a PSY isoform, it belongs to the first committed/upstream step of the carotenoid pathway; this is inferable from gene identity.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Supplying precursor pool for PSY3 specifically is inferred from homology/discussion, not directly tested in this paper.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The entry correctly says 'not directly established in this study,' which matches the paper for PSY3.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Although expected for a PSY enzyme, PSY3-specific substrate usage is not directly shown in this paper.

Primary_Product.reason: Phytoene production by PSY3 is inferred from identity as a PSY isoform, not directly tested here.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The specific PSY3 reaction is not directly demonstrated in this paper, only inferred from its annotation as PSY3.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Carotenoids and strigolactone/apocarotenoid-related products are discussed for PSY3 mainly from prior work, not directly shown here.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Apocarotenoid linkage for PSY3 is discussed from previous studies, not experimentally established here.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Support for root/apocarotenoid and AM symbiosis function comes from cited literature/discussion rather than this paper's Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results/discussion indicate PSY3 transcripts are highest in roots and hardly detectable in shoots.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PSY3 is clearly a phytoene synthase isoform in the plastidial carotenoid pathway context.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results/discussion state PSY3 is hardly detectable in shoot tissues, highest in roots, and induced during AM colonization according to cited work.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Plastid localization is plausible for PSY enzymes, but not explicitly stated for PSY3 in this paper.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Supported by inference from double mutant albino phenotype and root ABA data: PSY3 does not detectably support shoot carotenoid biosynthesis or salt-induced root ABA under tested conditions.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These are indeed the approaches used to infer PSY3 role/lack thereof: double mutant genetics, expression analysis, and root ABA measurements.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Shoot development, HL leaves, and salt-stress roots are contexts discussed/tested relevant to PSY3 inference.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements are consistent with results/discussion, though based partly on inference rather than direct PSY3 mutant testing.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is speculative and not directly addressed experimentally.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Paper explicitly notes PSY3 is discussed as root-specialized and was not a primary experimental target with direct mutant analysis.
